+ /// Returns an async [`futures_util::Stream`] that yields every `AuditLogAction`
224
+ /// across all pages, advancing the `after` cursor under the hood.
225
+ ///
226
+ /// ```ignore
227
+ /// use futures_util::TryStreamExt;
228
+ /// let all: Vec<AuditLogAction> = self
229
+ /// .list_actions_auto_paging(params)
230
+ /// .try_collect()
231
+ /// .await?;
232
+ /// ```
233
+ pub fn list_actions_auto_paging(
234
+ &self,
235
+ params: ListActionsParams,
236
+ ) -> impl futures_util::Stream<Item = Result<AuditLogAction, Error>> + '_ {
237
+ crate::pagination::auto_paginate_pages(move |after| {
238
+ let mut params = params.clone();
239
+ params.after = after;
240
+ async move {
241
+ let page = self.list_actions(params).await?;
242
+ Ok((page.data, page.list_metadata.after))
243
+ }
244
+ })
245
+ }

285
+ &self,
286

287

292

294

295

296

297

+ #[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize)]
205
+ pub struct ListGroupRoleAssignmentsParams {
206
+ /// An object ID that defines your place in the list. When the ID is not present, you are at the end of the list. For example, if you make a list request and receive 100 objects, ending with `"obj_123"`, your subsequent call can include `before="obj_123"` to fetch a new batch of objects before `"obj_123"`.
207
+ #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
208
+ pub before: Option<String>,
209
+ /// An object ID that defines your place in the list. When the ID is not present, you are at the end of the list. For example, if you make a list request and receive 100 objects, ending with `"obj_123"`, your subsequent call can include `after="obj_123"` to fetch a new batch of objects after `"obj_123"`.
210
+ #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
211
+ pub after: Option<String>,
212
+ /// Upper limit on the number of objects to return, between `1` and `100`.
213
+ ///
214
+ /// Defaults to `10`.
215
+ #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
216

202

203

204

205
+ .and(path_matcher(
206
+ "/authorization/groups/test_id/role_assignments",
207

219

220
+ ),
221
+ )

223

224
+ let api = match &err {
225
+ Error::Api(api) => api.as_ref(),
226
+ other => panic!("expected Error::Api, got {other:?}"),
227
+ };
228
+ assert_eq!(api.status, 401);
229
+ }

132
+ Error::Api(api) => api.as_ref(),
133
+ other => panic!("expected Error::Api, got {other:?}"),
134
+ };
135
+ assert_eq!(api.status, 500);
136
+ }
137
+
138
+ #[tokio::test]
139
+ async fn client_api_create_token_bad_request() {
140
+ let server = MockServer::start().await;
141
+ let template = ResponseTemplate::new(400)
142
+ .set_body_string("{\"code\":\"validation_error\",\"message\":\"Bad request\"}");
143
+ Mock::given(method("POST"))
144
+ .and(path_matcher("/client/token"))
145
+ .respond_with(template)
146
+ .expect(1)
147
+ .mount(&server)
148
+ .await;
149
+ let client = common::test_client(&server).await;
150
+ let err = client
151
+ .client_api()
152
+ .create_token(workos::client_api::CreateTokenParams::new(
153
+ serde_json::from_str(include_str!("fixtures/client_api_token.json"))
154
+ .expect("parse fixture for ClientApiToken"),
155
+ ))
156
+ .await
157
+ .expect_err("expected error");
158
+ let api = match &err {
159
+ Error::Api(api) => api.as_ref(),
160
+ other => panic!("expected Error::Api, got {other:?}"),
161
+ };
162
+ assert_eq!(api.status, 400);
163
+ assert_eq!(api.code.as_deref(), Some("validation_error"));
164
+ }
165
+
166
+ #[tokio::test]
167
+ async fn client_api_create_token_unprocessable() {
168
+ let server = MockServer::start().await;
169

170
+ Mock::given(method("POST"))
171
+ .and(path_matcher("/client/token"))
172
+ .respond_with(template)
173
+ .expect(1)
174
+ .mount(&server)
175
+ .await;
176
+ let client = common::test_client(&server).await;
177
+ let err = client
178
+ .client_api()
179
+ .create_token(workos::client_api::CreateTokenParams::new(
180
+ serde_json::from_str(include_str!("fixtures/client_api_token.json"))
181
+ .expect("parse fixture for ClientApiToken"),
182
+ ))
183
+ .await
184
+ .expect_err("expected error");
185
+ let api = match &err {
186
+ Error::Api(api) => api.as_ref(),
187
+ other => panic!("expected Error::Api, got {other:?}"),
188
+ };
189
+ assert_eq!(api.status, 422);
190
+ }

+ .list_organization_data_integration_configurations("test_id")
123
+ .await
124
+ .expect_err("expected error");
125
+ let api = match &err {
126
+ Error::Api(api) => api.as_ref(),
127
+ other => panic!("expected Error::Api, got {other:?}"),
128
+ };
129
+ assert_eq!(api.status, 500);
130
+ }
131

+ .await;

+ #[tokio::test]
221
+ async fn pipes_provider_update_organization_data_integration_configuration_rate_limited() {
222
+ let server = MockServer::start().await;
223

224
+ .insert_header("retry-after", "1")
225
+ .set_body_string("{\"message\":\"Slow down\"}");
226
+ Mock::given(method("PUT"))
227
+ .and(path_matcher(
228
+ "/organizations/test_id/data_integration_configurations/test_id",
229
+ ))
230
+ .respond_with(template)
231
+ .expect(1)
232
+ .mount(&server)
233
+ .await;
234
+ let client = common::test_client(&server).await;
235
+ let err = client
236
+ .pipes_provider()
237
+ .update_organization_data_integration_configuration(
238
+ "test_id",
239
+ "test_id",
240
+ workos::pipes_provider::UpdateOrganizationDataIntegrationConfigurationParams::new(
241
+ serde_json::from_str("{}").expect("parse stub for ConfigureDataIntegrationBody"),
242
+ ),
243
+ )
244
+ .await
245
+ .expect_err("expected error");
246
+ let api = match &err {
247
+ Error::Api(api) => api.as_ref(),
248
+ other => panic!("expected Error::Api, got {other:?}"),
249
+ };
250
+ assert_eq!(api.status, 429);
251
+ assert_eq!(api.retry_after, Some(std::time::Duration::from_secs(1)));
252
+ }
